attorneys.biography:
Cassie Murphy is a Partner at the Law Offices of Paone, Zaleski & Murphy, where she focuses her practice on divorce, child custody, and family law. She is one of fewer than 200 attorneys in New Jersey designated as a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney by the New Jersey Supreme Court — a distinction requiring demonstrated experience, a rigorous examination, and peer recognition. Ms. Murphy has also been appointed by the Supreme Court of New Jersey to the Matrimonial Law Certification Committee for 2025 and 2026. She is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, one of only approximately 60 family law attorneys in New Jersey selected to the nation's most prestigious matrimonial law bar association. She is admitted to practice in New Jersey, New York, and the District of Columbia.

Ms. Murphy is an active leader in the New Jersey legal community, serving as Vice Chair of the Family Law Executive Committee of the New Jersey State Bar Association for the 2026–2027 term and as an appointed member of both the NJSBA Amicus Committee and the Monmouth County Family Law Committee. She is an Associate Editor of New Jersey Family Lawyer and co-authored the second edition of "Divorce in New Jersey." Ms. Murphy has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year from 2020 to 2026, named to the Top 50 Women Super Lawyers in New Jersey for 2025 and 2026, and included in The Best Lawyers in America since 2025. She received the Martin Goldin Award for excellence in family law in 2018. Ms. Murphy earned her Bachelor of Arts from the University of Maryland, graduating summa cum laude, and her Juris Doctor from the University of Maryland School of Law, graduating cum laude.
attorneys.meta_description: Cassie Murphy, NJ Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow, handles complex divorce and custody cases in Red Bank and Monmouth County.

attorneys.biography:
Megan S. Murray is the founder of The Family Law Offices of Megan S. Murray in Holmdel, New Jersey, where she devotes her practice exclusively to family law matters including divorce, alimony, child support, equitable distribution, child custody, and high-net-worth cases. She is Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, where she serves on the Board of Managers. Megan is also a member of the Matrimonial Lawyers Alliance, an organization limited to 50 family law attorneys in New Jersey.

Megan has earned widespread recognition among New Jersey's top family law attorneys, earning Super Lawyers distinction from 2019 through 2026 and selection to Best Lawyers in America beginning in 2019. She was named a New Leader of the Bar by the New Jersey Law Journal in 2015 and received the Martin Goldin Award for excellence in family law in 2012. Professionally, she has served as Chair of the Family Law Section of the New Jersey State Bar Association (2023-2024), Secretary of that section (2019-2020), and Associate Managing Editor of the New Jersey Family Lawyer. Megan earned her Bachelor of Arts, summa cum laude, from Boston University and her Juris Doctorate from Wake Forest University School of Law in 2005.
attorneys.meta_description: Megan Murray, AAML Fellow and founder of her Holmdel firm, has guided Monmouth County families through divorce and high-net-worth cases since 2005.

attorneys.biography:
Eric S. Solotoff is a partner at Fox Rothschild LLP in Morristown, New Jersey, where his practice concentrates exclusively on family law litigation, mediation, and arbitration. He is Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers. His caseload spans complex matrimonial actions involving business valuation and high-conflict disputes, custody and parenting time, alimony and child support, equitable distribution, prenuptial and postnuptial agreements, palimony and cohabitant rights, domestic violence, and appellate practice. He has been involved in several precedent-setting New Jersey family law decisions, including Gnall v. Gnall on alimony duration and Maeker v. Ross on palimony, and he also serves as a mediator and arbitrator for parties seeking alternative dispute resolution in divorce matters.

Solotoff is ranked Band 1 by Chambers and Partners for High-Net-Worth Family/Matrimonial law in New Jersey, has been named to The Best Lawyers in America for Family Law since 2019, and has appeared on the Super Lawyers list continuously since 2007. He was appointed by the New Jersey Supreme Court to the District XB Ethics Committee, which he chaired from 2010 to 2011, and he is active in the American Bar Association, the New Jersey State Bar Association, and the Essex and Morris County Bar Associations. He edits Fox Rothschild's New Jersey Family Law Blog. Solotoff earned his B.A. cum laude from the State University of New York at Albany in 1989, his J.D. from Widener University School of Law in 1992, and an LL.M. in Taxation from Widener in 1994.
attorneys.meta_description: Eric Solotoff, Chambers Band 1-ranked matrimonial attorney at Fox Rothschild in Morristown, has litigated precedent-setting NJ family law cases since 1992.

attorneys.biography:
Francesca O'Cathain is the managing partner of O'Cathain Law Group in Hackensack, New Jersey, where her practice is devoted to divorce, matrimonial, and family law. She is board-certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney — a credential held by only about 3% of New Jersey attorneys — and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, one of roughly 45 New Jersey family lawyers admitted to that organization. Her practice concentrates on high-asset and high-income divorce cases that proceed through trial, contentious custody disputes, and divorces involving children with special needs; she is also known in the Bergen County law enforcement and education communities for handling cases involving the pension and retirement benefits unique to those professions. She is Of Counsel to the New York firm Berkman, Bottger, Newman & Schein.

O'Cathain has been selected to the Super Lawyers list for New Jersey Family Law from 2020 to 2026, following several years on the Super Lawyers Rising Stars list. She currently serves as Past President of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers' New Jersey Chapter, and is active in the New Jersey State Bar Association, the Bergen County Bar Association, and multiple Irish-American legal associations, including as a founding member and former treasurer of the Irish American Bar Association of New Jersey. She frequently lectures on family law topics involving children with special needs and has received an Award of Merit from the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children for her work on Hague Convention cases. O'Cathain earned her B.A. from Claremont McKenna College in 2001 and her J.D. from the University of Connecticut School of Law in 2004.
attorneys.meta_description: Francesca O'Cathain, NJ Certified Matrimonial Attorney in Hackensack, focuses on high-asset divorce and special-needs custody cases in Bergen County.

attorneys.biography:
Patricia M. Barbarito is a Senior Partner and member of the Board of Directors and Executive Committee at Einhorn, Barbarito, Frost, Botwinick, Nunn & Musmanno, PC in Parsippany, where she has practiced since 1980. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, she represents high-profile and high-net-worth individuals, including corporate executives, in complex divorces, prenuptial agreements, and asset division matters. She has been ranked in the Chambers High Net Worth guide for New Jersey Family/Matrimonial law (Band 2) every year since 2022 and holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell.

Ms. Barbarito has been listed in The Best Lawyers in America since 2006 and named to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2005, including repeated inclusion on the New Jersey Top 50 Women list. She was named among the New York Times' Top Ten Leaders in Matrimonial and Divorce Law in Northern New Jersey and received the New Jersey State Bar Association's Saul A. Tischler Award for her contributions to matrimonial law. She has chaired the NJSBA's Family Law Section and served on its Executive Committee, and represented Morris County on the NJSBA's Judicial and Prosecutorial Appointment Committee. She earned her Bachelor of Arts, magna cum laude, and her Juris Doctor from Seton Hall University.
attorneys.meta_description: Patricia Barbarito is a Chambers-ranked, Certified Matrimonial Attorney handling high-net-worth divorce cases in Morris County, New Jersey.

attorneys.biography:
Charles C. Abut is Of Counsel at Sarno da Costa D’Aniello Maceri Webb LLC in Hackensack, where he concentrates his practice on complex divorce litigation, arbitration, and mediation. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers. A court-accredited mediator through the New Jersey Administrative Office of the Courts, Mr. Abut has mediated and arbitrated hundreds of matrimonial and business disputes and has litigated numerous cases resulting in reported appellate decisions, including Piscopo v. Piscopo and National Westminster Bank v. Gilchinsky, which established New Jersey's “badges of fraud” precedent for fraudulent conveyance claims.

Mr. Abut has been recognized in The Best Lawyers in America since 2001, earning “Lawyer of the Year” honors for Family Law Mediation in Hackensack in 2025 and for Family Law in Hackensack in 2018 and 2024. He has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2005 and holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell, the organization's highest peer-review distinction. A frequent lecturer for the New Jersey Institute for Continuing Legal Education and the New Jersey Judicial College, he has published articles in the New Jersey Law Journal and been quoted by The New York Times, The Wall Street Journal, and other national media. Mr. Abut earned his Bachelor of Arts from Columbia University, graduating cum laude, and his Juris Doctor from Cornell University Law School.
attorneys.meta_description: Charles Abut is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow handling high-conflict divorce and mediation cases in Bergen County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Robert J. Adinolfi is a founding shareholder and managing partner of Adinolfi, Roberto & Burick, P.A. in Mount Laurel, where he has practiced family law for nearly five decades. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney, a distinction held by only a small fraction of the state's family law practitioners, and he handles the full range of matrimonial matters including divorce, custody, alimony, equitable distribution, prenuptial agreements, and complex tax issues arising from marital dissolution. He litigated the landmark “engagement ring” case, Aronow v. Silver, which established the concept of no-fault engagement in New Jersey.

A Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers since 1989, Mr. Adinolfi served as President of its New Jersey Chapter from 2014 to 2015 after holding every other office on its board. He has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2006 and has been recognized in The Best Lawyers in America for eight consecutive years. He is a longtime member of the Camden County Bar Association and serves on the Family Law Executive Committee of the New Jersey State Bar Association. Mr. Adinolfi earned his Bachelor of Science in Accounting, cum laude, from Saint Joseph's University and his Juris Doctor from Rutgers University School of Law in Camden.
attorneys.meta_description: Robert Adinolfi is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow with nearly 50 years handling divorce and custody cases in Camden County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Allison Ansell chairs the Matrimonial & Family Law Department at Ansell Grimm & Aaron, PC in Ocean, New Jersey, a group of six attorneys handling the full spectrum of divorce and family law matters. Following law school, she served as law clerk to the Honorable Alexander D. Lehrer, then the presiding judge of the Family Part of Monmouth County. She is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and a member of the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Executive Committee.

Ms. Ansell has been selected to the Super Lawyers list nearly every year since 2007 and was recognized in The Best Lawyers in America's 2026 edition. In 2019, she received the Bernard H. Hoffman Family Lawyer of the Year Award. She is active in the Monmouth County Bar Association, where she has served as a trustee and co-chaired the Family Law Committee, and previously served as President of Women Lawyers of Monmouth County and as a member of the Monmouth County District Ethics Committee. Ms. Ansell earned her Bachelor of Arts in Criminal Justice from American University and her Juris Doctor from Western New England College School of Law.
attorneys.meta_description: Allison Ansell chairs the family law department at Ansell Grimm & Aaron, handling divorce and custody matters throughout Ocean County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Mary Ann Bauer is a founding partner of Bauer & Karch Law Group, LLC in Lebanon, where she has practiced family law since graduating from Seton Hall University School of Law in 1988. She is a certified matrimonial attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, and she is also a trained collaborative law attorney, mediator, and certified arbitrator, giving clients multiple paths to resolve disputes outside of traditional litigation. Her practice covers divorce, custody and parenting time, alimony, child support, and domestic violence matters throughout Hunterdon County and the surrounding area.

Ms. Bauer has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2015 and has been repeatedly named to the Top 50 Women New Jersey Super Lawyers list. In 2014, she received the Hunterdon County Bar Association's Professional Lawyer of the Year Award. She also serves the family court system as an appointed parenting coordinator and mediator. Ms. Bauer earned her Bachelor of Science with honors from Rutgers University and her Juris Doctor, cum laude, from Seton Hall University School of Law.
attorneys.meta_description: Mary Ann Bauer is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow providing divorce and mediation services in Hunterdon County, NJ.
